examples: While it has never been illegal to be a Bahai in Egypt , it has never been easy.
The population is 89% Shia Muslim, 10% Sunni (mostly Kurds) and 1% Zoroastrian, Bahai, Jewish or Christian.
The Bahai faith is regarded as heretical by Iran's Shia establishment.
Some reports claimed they had converted from Islam to Christianity or Bahai faith - potentially a capital offence in Iran as it is considered to be apostasy.
